items.claim: Anthropic annualized revenue $19B (March 2026), up 1,167% YoY
items.notes: Sacra estimate. NOTE: This is annualized run rate, NOT collected revenue. Our Sankey uses actual collected. Previous Sacra data shows $4.5B for 2025, $14B ARR in Feb 2026. Growth from $875M ARR Jan 2025.

items.claim: Anthropic 2025 actual revenue confirmed ~$4.5B (Sacra historical)
items.notes: Sacra confirms our existing $4.5B figure for Anthropic 2025 revenue. Cross-referenced with The Information reporting. Validates current Sankey number.

items.claim: OpenAI annualized revenue ~$25B (March 2026)
items.notes: Sacra states OpenAI at $25B annualized from $13.1B. NOTE: ARR not collected. Our Sankey had $6B collected for 2025. Need to reconcile ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ The Information previously reported $13.1B which may be 2025 actual collected.

items.claim: Claude Code annualized revenue >$2.5B (Feb 2026)
items.notes: Claude Code hit $2.5B ARR in Feb 2026, doubled since start of 2026 (was $500M ARR in Sep 2025). Major new revenue stream ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ not broken out in our current Sankey.

items.claim: Anthropic expects cash flow breakeven by 2028, burned ~$3B in 2025
items.notes: Anthropic burned ~$3B in 2025 (down from $5.6B prior year). Our Sankey had $5.2B operating loss. $3B cash burn is lower ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ may reflect different accounting (cash vs operating loss). $80B cloud infra costs projected through 2029.

items.claim: Anthropic: 70-75% revenue from API, 10-15% from subscriptions
items.notes: Revenue breakdown: 70-75% token-based API, 10-15% subscriptions, remainder reserved capacity/enterprise. Useful for Sankey channel allocation (Model Subs vs API vs Enterprise).

items.claim: Anthropic Series G: $30B raised at $380B valuation (Feb 2026)
items.notes: Series G closed Feb 2026. Led by GIC and Coatue. Amazon total investment $8B. Up from $183B valuation in Sep 2025.

items.claim: Anthropic has 300,000+ business customers, 500 spending >$1M/yr
items.notes: As of Oct 2025: 300K+ biz customers (~80% of revenue), 500 spending >$1M/yr (up from 12 two years ago). 8 of Fortune 10 are Claude customers.

items.claim: Claude Code: 29M daily VS Code installs, 4% of public GitHub commits
items.notes: Claude Code developer adoption metrics. 29M daily VS Code installs. 4% of all public GitHub commits.

items.claim: Gemini: 650M monthly users vs ChatGPT 800M weekly users
items.notes: User comparison: Gemini 650M monthly vs ChatGPT 800M weekly (note different measurement periods). Google boosted by Nano Banana launch.

items.claim: OpenAI 2024 actual collected revenue ~$3.6B (from MSFT rev share docs)
items.notes: Reverse-engineered from MSFT rev share payments ($493.8M / 0.20 = ~$2.5B, but net of MSFTÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂOAI payments pushes higher). Far below $4B+ ARR figures leaked to press.

items.claim: OpenAI H1 2025 actual collected revenue ~$4.3B
items.notes: The Information reported $4.3B H1 2025 revenue. Zitron's MSFT rev share analysis implies it may be closer to $2.27B ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ a ~$2B gap. Used $4.3B as the more conservative (higher) figure.

items.claim: OpenAI full-year 2025 estimated collected revenue ~$11.9B (Zitron extrapolation)
items.notes: Zitron's full-year 2025 estimate assuming linear growth from Jan-Sep data. Below Altman's claim of 'well more than $13B' and far below $20B ARR figure. Our original Sankey had $6B (too low). The Information reported $13.1B.

items.claim: OpenAI H1 2025 inference spend $5.0B (exceeds H1 revenue)
items.notes: OpenAI spent $5.022B on inference in H1 2025 alone ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ MORE than $4.3B H1 revenue. Full 2024-Sep2025 inference total: $12.4B. This is a critical data point: inference costs EXCEED revenue for OpenAI.

items.claim: OpenAI spending ratio: $2.25-$2.60 to make $1 in revenue
items.notes: OpenAI spending $2.25-$2.60 for every $1 earned. Implies ~60% of total spend is just inference, rest is R&D ($6.7B H1), sales/marketing ($2B H1), and other ops.

items.claim: OpenAI $25B ARR uses best 4-week period ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 12 (not monthly ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 12)
items.notes: CRITICAL: OpenAI's $25B ARR confirmed to use best 4-week period ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 12, NOT calendar month ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 12. This inflates the figure by an estimated 10-15% vs monthly calculation. Must apply cherry-pick haircut in ARR conversion.

items.claim: OpenAI ads pilot hit $100M ARR in under 2 months
items.notes: OpenAI ad pilot revenue $100M ARR within 2 months of launch. New revenue stream not in current Sankey. Adds to total ARR but note ARR methodology caveat.

items.claim: OpenAI funding round extended to $120B total at $840B post-money valuation (March 2026)
items.notes: OpenAI added extra $10B to existing round, bringing total to $120B at $840B post-money valuation. Significantly higher than Anthropic's $380B valuation.

items.claim: OpenAI projected 2026 cash burn ~$25B
items.notes: OpenAI projected to burn ~$25B in 2026 even as revenue hits $25B ARR. Implies close to breakeven on ARR basis but not on actual collected revenue. Critical context for burn rate analysis.

items.claim: OpenAI internal 2030 revenue forecast: $280B
items.notes: OpenAI's internal 2030 revenue forecast is $280B. Context: current 2025 actual ~$13B, implies 21x growth in 5 years. Highly speculative ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ flagged for perspective/context use only.

items.claim: Microsoft AI business $13B ARR (Q2 FY2026, Jan 2026); Copilot 15M paid seats, 3.3% of M365 commercial base
items.notes: Microsoft AI at $13B ARR Q2 FY2026. Copilot: 15M paid seats (+160% YoY) = 3.3% of 450M commercial M365 base. Key enterprise adoption data point ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 2+ years in, still only 1-in-30 users paying.

items.claim: Salesforce Agentforce standalone Q4 FY2026 ARR: $800M (+169% YoY); combined AI + Data 360 FY2026: $2.9B (+200% YoY)
items.notes: Salesforce Agentforce Q4 FY2026: $800M ARR standalone (+169% YoY). Full FY2026 AI + Data 360 combined: $2.9B (+200% YoY). Also: 19 trillion tokens processed (5x YoY). Official earnings release.

items.claim: ServiceNow Now Assist ACV $600M+ (Q4 2025), doubled YoY; FY2026 subscription revenue guidance $15.53-15.57B (+20% YoY)
items.notes: Now Assist ACV $600M+ at Q4 2025, doubled YoY (tracking toward $1B+ for 2026). Total ServiceNow FY2026 sub revenue guided at $15.53-15.57B. AI = ~4% of total sub base ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ consistent with low-penetration thesis.

items.claim: Harvey (legal AI) raised $200M at $11B valuation (March 25, 2026)
items.notes: Harvey raised $200M at $11B valuation. Legal AI vertical ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ notable as sector-specific AI company reaching unicorn+ status.

items.claim: Physical Intelligence (ex-DeepMind robotics) in talks for ~$1B round at $11B+ valuation (March 27, 2026)
items.notes: Physical Intelligence (robotics startup, ex-DeepMind staff) in talks for ~$1B at $11B+ valuation. AI investment extending into physical AI/robotics sector.

items.claim: Anthropic 2026 internal revenue forecast raised 20% to $18B
items.notes: Anthropic raised its internal 2026 revenue forecast by 20% to $18B. Note: this is a forecast for actual collected revenue (not ARR), distinct from the $19B current ARR run rate.

items.claim: February 2026 was largest startup funding month ever: $189B globally; AI startups = 41% of all VC dollars
items.notes: Feb 2026 record driven by OpenAI $110B + Anthropic $30B + Waymo $16B. AI = 41% of all venture dollars on Carta. Highly concentrated ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 3 deals = ~83% of the record month.

items.claim: OpenAI projected operating loss $14B in 2026 (tripling from ~$5B in 2025)
items.notes: OpenAI internal projections imply $14B operating loss in 2026 ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ tripling from ~$5B in 2025. Burn rate ~57% of projected revenue. Critical context: losses growing faster than revenue.

items.claim: OpenAI cumulative cash burn $115B through 2029
items.notes: OpenAI says its business will burn $115B cumulatively through 2029. Projects profitability at $100B revenue in 2029. Contrasts with Anthropic target of 9% burn rate by 2027.

items.claim: OpenAI burn rate 57% of revenue in 2026-2027; Anthropic burn dropping to 9% of revenue by 2027
items.notes: OpenAI burn 57% of revenue through 2027. Anthropic projects burn dropping to ~33% in 2026 and 9% by 2027 ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ structural divergence in economics between the two leading frontier labs.

items.claim: Anthropic gross margin projection lowered ~10 percentage points vs prior forecast due to rising inference costs
items.notes: Anthropic cut gross margin projection by ~10pp as cloud server/inference costs outpaced revenue growth. Both OpenAI and Anthropic missed their own gross margin forecasts. Signals compute cost pressure despite rapid revenue growth.

items.claim: Anthropic cash flow breakeven timeline delayed beyond 2028 (previously projected 2028)
items.notes: Anthropic raised 2026 revenue forecast 20% to $18B but simultaneously delayed cash flow positive timeline past 2028. Revenue growth not translating to faster profitability path.

items.claim: OpenRouter March 2026: MiniMax M2.5 #1 by token volume (4.55T tokens), Moonshot Kimi K2.5 #2 (4.02T tokens); Chinese models ~2/3 of top-5 token consumption
items.notes: Chinese models dominate OpenRouter by token volume in March 2026. MiniMax M2.5: 4.55T tokens (#1), Kimi K2.5: 4.02T (#2), DeepSeek #4. Chinese models = ~2/3 of top-5 consumption. Top-5 includes only one Anthropic model.

items.claim: OpenRouter processing 1T+ tokens per day as of March 2026; 100T+ tokens/year total throughput
items.notes: OpenRouter processing 1T+ tokens/day (March 2026). Annual run rate: 100T+ tokens/year. Now routes traffic across 300+ models from 60+ providers, serving 5M+ developers. Key demand-side indicator.

items.claim: H100 cloud GPU spot pricing fell to ~$2.99/hr in Q1 2026, down 64-75% from $8-10/hr in Q4 2024
items.notes: H100 spot pricing ~$2.99/hr (Q1 2026) vs $8-10/hr (Q4 2024). Specialized GPU providers 60-85% cheaper than hyperscalers at $0.29-$2.99/hr.

items.claim: LLM inference cost collapsed ~1,000x: GPT-4-class model $20/M tokens (late 2022) ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ ~$0.40/M tokens (early 2026)
items.notes: From $20/M tokens (Nov 2022) to $0.40/M tokens (early 2026) for GPT-4 class performance ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ a ~1,000x reduction in 3 years. Driven by hardware advances, competition, efficiency gains.

items.claim: Inference accounts for ~2/3 of all AI compute workloads in 2026, overtaking training as the dominant workload
items.notes: Inference now ~2/3 of all AI compute (up from minority share in 2023). Training has been overtaken as the dominant AI workload ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ structurally important for GPU demand forecasting.

items.claim: Qwen (Alibaba) 400M+ Hugging Face downloads; Chinese open models overtook US models in total hub downloads
items.notes: Qwen: 400M+ downloads; OpenAI/DeepSeek ~50M each. 113K+ Qwen derivative models. China = 41% of Hugging Face downloads. Chinese open models now dominant on largest ML hub.

items.claim: Google Cloud Q4 2025 revenue $17.66B (+47.8% YoY); $240B cloud backlog (doubled YoY) heading into 2026
items.notes: Google Cloud Q4 2025: $17.66B (+47.8% YoY). Full-year 2025 growth accelerated through H2. $240B backlog doubled YoY ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ indicates committed future AI cloud revenue. Vertex AI cited as key driver.

items.claim: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 API repriced to $5/$25 per M input/output tokens; long-context (1M token) surcharge eliminated for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
items.notes: Opus 4.6: $5/$25 per M in/out tokens (was higher; ~67% cut). Sonnet 4.6: $3/$15. Haiku 4.5: $1/$5. Long-context surcharge removed ÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ 1M context window now at standard per-token rates.

items.claim: OpenRouter model rankings by token volume: Anthropic #1 (1.1T tokens, -1.6%/wk), OpenAI #2 (382B tokens, +19.3%/wk), Google #3 (323B tokens, +1.9%/wk)
items.notes: OpenRouter live rankings snapshot Apr 2026. Anthropic leads with 1.1T total but declining -1.6%/wk. OpenAI surging +19.3%/wk (382B tokens). OSS models ~1/3 of usage; Chinese OSS near 30% some weeks.

items.claim: Nscale (AI compute infrastructure) raised $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation (Q1 2026)
items.notes: Nscale $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation. AI compute infra now a sovereign-level investment priority. Signals large-scale private capital flowing into GPU/inference infrastructure beyond the frontier labs.

items.claim: Nvidia AI inference market share projected to fall from 90%+ today to 20-30% by 2028 as TPUs/ASICs capture 70-75% of production inference
items.notes: Analyst forecast: Nvidia falls from 90%+ inference share to 20-30% by 2028. Google TPUs 4.7x better perf/dollar and 67% lower power vs H100s. Highly speculative but directionally important.

items.notes: Crunchbase Q1 2026 recap. Record $297B into 6,000 startups globally. Up ~150% YoY. AI dominant driver. Single-quarter total near 70% of all 2025 VC.

items.claim: AI startups captured 81% of global VC in Q1 2026 (~$242B of $297B total)
items.notes: 81% of Q1 2026 global VC went to AI. Four frontier labs (OpenAI $120B, Anthropic $30B, xAI $20B, Waymo $16B) = $186B = 64% of all global VC for the quarter.

items.claim: xAI raised $20B in Q1 2026
items.notes: Elon Musk's xAI raised $20B in Q1 2026, per Crunchbase Q1 2026 funding recap. Part of the $186B raised by top 4 frontier labs in Q1.

items.claim: Chinese AI models hit 61% of total token consumption on OpenRouter (Feb 2026)

items.claim: Programming use cases grew from 11% to >50% of OpenRouter total token volume throughout 2025

items.claim: OpenAI funding round final close: $122B raised at $852B post-money valuation (March 31, 2026)
items.notes: Round closed at $852B (vs $840B preliminary in scan-20260330-b2c). $122B total (vs $120B preliminary). Amazon $50B, Nvidia $30B, SoftBank $30B, a16z. Retail tranche: $3B. IPO expected 2026.

items.claim: OpenAI generating $2B/month revenue as of late March 2026 (~$24B annualized)
items.notes: Self-reported at funding round close. $2B/month = ~$24B annualized. 2025 actual was $13.1B. Note: OpenAI ARR methodology uses best 4-week window x12 (dp-064) which may inflate vs monthly figure.

items.claim: Meta Llama cumulative downloads: 1B (March 2025), updated to 1.2B (April 2025)
items.notes: 1B downloads announced March 2025, updated to 1.2B at April 2025 LlamaCon. Dwarfs Qwen (400M+) and DeepSeek (~50M) cumulative figures. Majority are Llama derivative models.

items.claim: OpenAI projects $111B additional cash burn through 2030; 2025 actual burn ~$8B (below $9B target)
items.notes: Updated forecast: $111B more burn through 2030. Compare to prior scan-20260331-b2c ($115B through 2029). Also: 2025 actual burn $8B (better than $9B target). Revenue forecasts raised alongside.

items.claim: OpenRouter weekly token rankings (Apr 7, 2026): Google #1 (26.6%), Anthropic #2 (18.5%), DeepSeek #3 (16.7%), Qwen #4 (10.3%), OpenAI #5 (10.2%)
items.notes: Major shift from Apr 1 snapshot (scan-20260401-g7h): Google now #1 at 26.6%, Anthropic fell to #2 (18.5%). DeepSeek #3 (16.7%), Qwen #4 (10.3%), OpenAI #5 (10.2%). OSS models gaining rapidly.

items.claim: Microsoft pledges $5.5B AI investment in Singapore through 2029 for cloud and AI infrastructure
items.notes: Microsoft $5.5B into Singapore cloud/AI infra through 2029. Includes free AI tools for students. Part of Microsoft's broader APAC AI expansion alongside India ($17.5B) and Japan ($10B).

items.claim: Microsoft drafts $10B AI investment plan in Japan (announced April 3, 2026)
items.notes: Microsoft planning $10B investment in Japan AI/cloud infrastructure. Follows Singapore $5.5B (Apr 1). Microsoft APAC AI infra commitments now exceed $27B (India $17.5B + Japan $10B + Singapore $5.5B).

items.claim: Anthropic gross margin: -94% in 2024, turned to ~+40% in 2025; targets 50% in 2026 and 77% in 2028
items.notes: Anthropic gross margin swung from -94% (2024) to ~+40% (2025). Targets 50% (2026) and 77% (2028). Pressured by inference costs. Vault has -10pp revision (scan-20260331-e5f) but not the baseline -94% figure.

items.claim: Anthropic revenue run rate topped $30B (April 6, 2026), up from $9B at end of 2025
items.notes: Anthropic ARR hit $30B as of April 6, up 3x+ from $9B at end of 2025 and up from $19B in March (email-20260328-a1x). Bloomberg confirmed alongside Broadcom compute deal announcement.

items.claim: Anthropic: 1,000+ business customers spending >$1M/yr as of April 2026 (more than doubled since February)
items.notes: 1,000+ customers at $1M+/yr (April 2026), more than doubling since February. Vault previously had 500 (Oct 2025, email-20260328-h8s). Significant enterprise acceleration.

items.claim: Anthropic signs 3.5GW TPU compute deal with Google and Broadcom; Mizuho estimates $21B AI revenue for Broadcom from Anthropic in 2026, $42B in 2027
items.unit: $B (Broadcom AI revenue from Anthropic, 2026 Mizuho est.)
items.notes: Broadcom to supply 1GW of Google TPU capacity to Anthropic in 2026, expanding to 3.5GW in 2027. Mizuho: $21B Broadcom AI revenue from Anthropic in 2026, $42B in 2027. Confirms shift away from Nvidia GPUs.

items.claim: Microsoft Copilot ToS updated to 'entertainment purposes only'; not for professional or business-critical use (April 5, 2026)
items.notes: Microsoft ToS (updated Oct 2025, widely noticed April 2026): Copilot is 'for entertainment purposes only', use 'at your own risk', 'not designed for professional or business-critical applications'. Microsoft says it will update the 'legacy language'. Directly supports low-enterprise-adoption thesis.

items.claim: China AI models surpassed US on OpenRouter for 5 consecutive weeks; week of Apr 6 saw 12.96T tokens from Chinese models, +31.48% WoW
items.notes: Chinese models topped all 6 spots on OpenRouter by token volume week of Mar 30-Apr 5. China at 12.96T tokens Apr 6 week, +31.48% WoW. 5 consecutive weeks surpassing US.

items.claim: H100 cloud GPU pricing further declined to under $2.50/hr on specialist providers in April 2026; A100s approaching commodity pricing below $1/hr
items.notes: H100s that launched above $7/hr on AWS now under $2.50/hr on specialist providers. A100s below $1/hr. Extends accepted data point (scan-20260401-a1b) showing further decline from $2.99/hr Q1 2026.

items.claim: OpenRouter platform processes 20T+ tokens per week as of April 2026, up from 1T+ tokens/day (7T/week) cited in March 2026
items.notes: If accurate, ~3x growth vs March 2026 (scan-20260331-g7h shows 1T+/day). Source is secondary; should verify against openrouter.ai directly.

items.claim: Anthropic ARR surpassed $30B (April 7, 2026), overtaking OpenAI at $25B
items.notes: Anthropic crossed $30B run-rate revenue in April 2026, first time it has outearned OpenAI. Up from $19B ARR in March 2026. Multiple outlets confirmed: Seeking Alpha, CNBC context, Whalesbook, TrendingTopics.

items.claim: Anthropic enterprise customers spending >$1M/yr doubled to 1,000+ between Feb and Apr 2026
items.notes: When Series G closed (Feb 2026), 500 customers spending >$1M/yr. By April 2026, that doubled to 1,000+. Vault previously had 500 figure (Oct 2025). Critical enterprise adoption velocity signal.

items.claim: Anthropic signed multi-gigawatt TPU agreement with Google and Broadcom; capacity online from 2027
items.notes: Anthropic + Google + Broadcom expanding chip deal: next-gen TPU capacity (multi-gigawatt scale) coming online 2027. Signals Anthropic pivoting away from Nvidia GPUs for inference at scale.

items.claim: NVIDIA Blackwell reduces inference cost per M tokens: 20Â¢ (Hopper H100) â 10Â¢ (Blackwell) â 5Â¢ (Blackwell NVFP4)
items.notes: DeepInfra example on open models: H100 Hopper 20Â¢/M tokens â Blackwell 10Â¢ (2x reduction) â Blackwell NVFP4 5Â¢ (4x reduction from Hopper). Confirmed by VentureBeat and NetworkWorld coverage.

items.claim: ATOM Report (Apr 2026): Qwen ~1B cumulative HuggingFace downloads by March 2026; Qwen derivative model share 69%
items.notes: Academic paper measuring open LLM ecosystem. Total 2.04B cumulative downloads by March 2026. Qwen: ~1B downloads (most of any org); derivative share 69% of all open model derivatives. Meta Llama fell from 25% to 11% derivative share.

items.claim: ATOM Report: Meta Llama OpenRouter inference share collapsed from 37.4% peak (Jan 2025) to ~0% by Jan 2026; DeepSeek 31.1%, Xiaomi 27.2%
items.notes: By Jan 2026 Meta Llama had zero OpenRouter inference market share (down from 37.4% peak in Jan 2025). Replaced by DeepSeek (31.1%) and Xiaomi (27.2%). Chinese models now dominate open model inference volume.
